item.text: The repo already documents the relational contract, so I’m reading the schema and DDL before changing code. That should tell me which columns the workflow needs to populate and whether there are any hidden expectations beyond the acceptance test.

item.aggregated_output:
# Schema Contract: Invoice dispute workflow

Lane: postgres

Business prompt:
Implement an invoice dispute workflow that combines account contract, invoice lines, order fulfillment, support evidence, payment history, owner approvals, and audit events.

Expected outcome:
Invoice dispute active with finance owner, evidence bundle, and customer-safe status.

Native data shape:
Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar

# Access Paths: Invoice dispute workflow

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: invoice

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.

README / documentation (full content)\\n\\n**README.md:**\\n> # Agent Schema Tax Benchmark V1: Strategic account rescue (postgres)\\n> This is a frozen target workspace for AST-Bench. The workflow is intentionally missing. The acceptance test must fail before a coding agent implements `src/workflow.mjs`. Run: `npm test` / `npm run render`\\n\\n**AGENTS.md** (the rules — load-bearing):\\n> - Implement only Strategic account rescue.\\n> - Use the existing normalized table-shaped data in data/.\\n> - Do not change tests.\\n> - Do not change generated fixture data.\\n> - Run `npm test` before finishing.\\n> - Keep the result behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.\\n\\n**schema/schema.md** — business prompt + object inventory. Business prompt: \\\"Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account... Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.\\\" Expected outcome: \\\"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.\\\" Native shape: \\\"Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.\\\" It lists row counts: `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` all start at **0 rows** (these are the tables the workflow must populate).\\n\\n**schema/access-paths.md** — the read/write path:\\n- Read: load workflow request → reconstruct account context → resolve owner groups + risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection.\\n- Write: persist ONE workflow state, ALL required owner tasks, ONE customer-safe message, ONE customer-visible audit event.\\n- Fairness rule: \\\"may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.\\\"\\n\\n## 4. Database schema / migrations\\n\\nThere is no live database. Two artifacts describe the schema:\\n\\n**migrations/001_table_contract.sql** — documentation-only DDL (never executed; no migration runner exists). Note the four target tables are declared with just a single `value text` column, but the actual JSON fixture stores real object rows — the SQL is a loose contract, the JSON is the source of truth. Tables: `benchmark_fixture`, `accounts`, `account_contracts`, `contacts`, `workflow_requests`, `workflow_request_owner_groups`, `workflow_request_risk_signals`, `activities`, and the four empty target tables `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`.\\n\\nThe real schema = the keys in `data/tables.json` (see section 8).\\n\\n## 5. applyBenchmarkTask } from \\\"../src/workflow.mjs\\\";\\nimport { buildPortalView } from \\\"../src/portal-view.mjs\\\";\\n\\nconst now = \\\"2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z\\\";\\nconst data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL(\\\"../data/tables.json\\\", import.meta.url), \\\"utf8\\\"));\\nconst before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));\\nstrictEqual(before.status, \\\"Needs work\\\", \\\"Before state starts without the workflow\\\");\\nstrictEqual(before.history, \\\"Not visible\\\", \\\"Before state has no customer-visible audit trail\\\");\\nstrictEqual(before.tasks, \\\"0 owner tasks\\\", \\\"Before state has no owner tasks\\\");\\n\\nconst runData = structuredClone(data);\\nconst after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);\\nconst expectedStatus = \\\"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.\\\";\\nconst expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);\\nconst expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: expectedOwners, expectedSignals);\\n\\nconsole.log(\\\"AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres\\\");\\n\\nfunction assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {\\n  \\n  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];\\n  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);\\n  strictEqual(Boolean(state), item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, \\\"Customer-visible audit event persisted\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### What the test demands (the implementation After it runs:\\n\\n**Returned object (`after`):**\\n- `after.status` === `\\\"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.\\\"` (the literal `expected_outcome` from the request).\\n- `after.owner` === owner groups sorted by `group_order`, joined with `\\\" + \\\"` → `\\\"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor\\\"`.\\n- `after.nextStep.length > 8` (any non-trivial string).\\n- `after.history` === `\\\"Audit visible\\\"`.\\n- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`.\\n- `after.riskSummary` === `\\\"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required\\\"` (count + signal names sorted by `signal_order`).\\n- `after.tasks` === `\\\"4 owner tasks\\\"`.\\n\\n**Persisted state (mutations to `runData`):**\\n- `workflow_state`: push one row with `request_id` === the request's, and `status` === expectedStatus.\\n- `owner_tasks`: one row per owner group, in `group_order` order, each with truthy `title`, truthy `due_at`, and `status === \\\"open\\\"`, and `owner_group` matching.\\n- `customer_messages`: at least one row with matching `request_id` and truthy `body`.\\n- `audit_events`: at least one row with matching `request_id` and truthy `customer_visible`.\\n\\nCrucially, `after` must reflect persisted state — the cleanest implementation persists rows into the tables then calls `buildPortalView(db)` (which is what `portal-view.mjs` reads). `buildPortalView` derives `status` from `workflow_state.status`, `owner`/`tasks` from `owner_tasks`, `history` from an `audit_events` row with `customer_visible`, `customerMessage` from `customer_messages.body`, and `riskSummary` from `workflow_request_risk_signals` (already present in fixture). diff --git a/src/workflow.mjs b/src/workflow.mjs
index bc890e5..948809e 100644
--- a/src/workflow.mjs
+++ b/src/workflow.mjs
@@ -1,6 +1,62 @@
 import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";
 
+const TASK_SLA_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
+
 export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
-  // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
+  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
+
+  // Owner routing and risk signals are already normalized; read them in their
+  // contracted order so persisted rows and the portal projection stay aligned.
+  const ownerGroups = db.workflow_request_owner_groups
+    .filter((group) => group.request_id === request.request_id)
+    .slice()
+    .sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order);
+  const signals = db.workflow_request_risk_signals
+    .filter((signal) => signal.request_id === request.request_id)
+    .slice()
+    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);
+  const evidence = db.activities.filter((activity) => activity.subject_id === request.request_id);
+  const dueAt = new Date(new Date(now).getTime() + TASK_SLA_MS).toISOString();
+
+  db.workflow_state.push({
+    request_id: request.request_id,
+    account_id: request.account_id,
+    title: request.title,
+    status: request.expected_outcome,
+    next_step: request.next_step,
+    risk_signal_count: signals.length,
+    evidence_count: evidence.length,
+    created_at: now,
+    updated_at: now
+  });
+
+  for (const group of ownerGroups) {
+    db.owner_tasks.push({
+      request_id: request.request_id,
+      owner_group: group.owner_group,
+      title: `${group.owner_group} review for ${request.title}`,
+      due_at: dueAt,
+      status: "open",
+      created_at: now
+    });
+  }
+
+  db.customer_messages.push({
+    request_id: request.request_id,
+    channel: "portal",
+    body: request.customer_message,
+    customer_safe: true,
+    created_at: now
+  });
+
+  db.audit_events.push({
+    request_id: request.request_id,
+    account_id: request.account_id,
+    event_type: "invoice_dispute_workflow_activated",
+    detail: `Routed to ${ownerGroups.map((group) => group.owner_group).join(", ")} with ${signals.length} risk signals and ${evidence.length} evidence activities.`,
+    customer_visible: true,
+    created_at: now
+  });
+
   return buildPortalView(db);
 }
